steps.title: Calculate Andrew's mean
steps.content: Andrew's six rock masses have sum $16.8$ kilograms, so Andrew's mean is $$\frac{16.8}{6}=2.8$$ kilograms.

steps.title: Set up Maria's mean equation
steps.content: Maria's mean must be $0.1$ greater than Andrew's, so Maria's mean is $2.9$. Since Maria has six rocks total, her required total is $6(2.9)=17.4$ kilograms.

steps.title: Solve for x
steps.content: Maria's known five masses total $14.8$ kilograms. Subtract that from the required total: $$17.4-14.8=2.6$$ The missing rock mass is 2.6 kilograms.
